member.name: Dr Caroline Johnson
member.role: MP for Sleaford and North Hykeham
member.synopsis: Dr Caroline Johnson is the Conservative MP for Sleaford and North Hykeham , and has been an MP continually since 8 December 2016. She currently undertakes the role of Shadow Minister (Health and Social Care).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: RAF Waddington: Housing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of the Defence Investment Plan on proposals to improve single living accommodation at RAF Waddington.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As set out in the Defence Investment Plan (DIP), the Ministry of Defence is investing £1.4 billion in the modernisation and renewal of Single Living Accommodation (SLA) as part of its commitment to creating a defence estate that better meets the needs of ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Defence: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of the Defence Investment Plan on Project Meharist.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Planning to deliver Project Meharist is progressing. Following publication of the Defence Investment Plan, the intention remains to deliver this critical Defence capability. The programme will continue to be kept under review to ensure it remains aligned ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS England: Reorganisation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what the cost to the public purse has been of the use of external management consultants in the planning and delivery of i) the restructuring of his Department, ii) the abolition of NHS England and
writtenQuestions.answerText: Expenditure on external consultants will be reported in our Annual Accounts in the normal way.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Healthwatch
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of ICB clustering on patient voice and the efficacy of Local Healthwatch.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Health Bill, currently before Parliament, proposes abolition of Healthwatch arrangements nationally and locally. This follows the publication of the report by Penny Dash published in July 2025, which concluded that even though there are multiple organ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Staff
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, which organisations, bodies, trade unions and other stakeholders have been (a) provided with advance sight of and (b) briefed on the contents of the forthcoming Workforce Plan.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We have undertaken an extensive programme of engagement to support the development the 10 Year Workforce Plan. This has included meeting with trade unions, education partners, Royal Colleges, think tanks, charities and system partners. This engagement has...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Prescriptions: Fees and Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many (a) adults and (b) children received drugs provided free of charge to the NHS for compassionate reasons in each year from 2021 to 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The information requested is not held centrally. Pharmaceutical companies can establish compassionate use or early access programmes through which medicines may be made available to National Health Service patients free of charge ahead of an NHS commissio...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Hospitals: Waiting Lists
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the impact of payments for validation on Trusts' willingness to validate waiting lists.
writtenQuestions.answerText: NHS England provided funding to support validation of waiting lists in 2025/26, as part of the Government's plans for a more productive and improved approach to elective care which is better for patients. Effective waiting list validation means trusts hav...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Placenta Accreta Spectrum: Diagnosis
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to improve early detection of placenta accreta.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Placenta Accreta Spectrum (PAS) is rare, but when it does occur it can cause bleeding that is potentially life-threatening. This is why maternity teams are trained to look for the warning signs and identify women at higher risk early in pregnancy.In 2020,...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Drugs: VAT
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, on what date HMRC began actively enforcing charges on VAT free of charge medicines.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Under long-standing UK VAT law, some non-monetary transactions are treated as supplies to ensure a fair tax result where VAT has been recovered on costs. The application of VAT to medicines or treatments provided free of charge depends on the specific fac...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS England
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the extent of disruption to the development of new services caused by the abolition of NHS England.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The abolition of NHS England is causing no disruption to the development of new services.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Drugs: Concessions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of VAT on the provision of free of charge compassionate drugs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the important role that expanded access programmes can play in enabling patients, particularly those with serious or rare conditions, to access innovative medicines outside routine commissioning arrangements.As with other goods, ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Syphilis
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to reduce incidence of syphilis.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK Health Security Agency published a national Syphilis Response Plan in March 2026, which is available at the following link:https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/syphilis-public-health-england-action-plan/ukhsa-syphilis-response-planThe plan fo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Electronic Cigarettes: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, when he plans to bring forward regulations on the (a) colour, (b) shape, (c) flavour and (d) display of (i) vaping and (ii) nicotine products.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It is incredibly worrying that 25% of 11- to 15-year-olds have tried vaping. That is why we are committed to bringing about definitive and positive change to stop future generations from becoming hooked on nicotine. Evidence shows us that vapes appeal to ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Staff
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, when he plans to publish the NHS workforce plan.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer I gave the hon. Member for Newton Abbot on 15 June 2026 to Question 7201.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Healthwatch: Expenditure

writtenQuestions.answerText: The following table shows the funding provided for Healthwatch England and Local Healthwatch. The funding shown for Local Healthwatch is only one element of the non-ringfenced funding provided to local authorities for Local Healthwatch, with the larger pr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Healthwatch: Cost Effectiveness
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what estimate he has made of the potential savings to the public purse of abolishing Healthwatch England and Local Healthwatch.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The potential savings from abolishing Healthwatch England and Local Healthwatch are dependent future policy decisions regarding the approach taken to collecting user experiences post-abolition. Our intention is to place responsibility for listening to pat...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Midwives: Training
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many students a) started and b) successfully completed a midwifery degree in i) 2025, ii) 2024, iii) 2023, iv) 2022, v) 2021, vi) 2020 and vii) 2019.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The number of starters on midwifery courses in England is collected and published by the Office for Students in its Higher Education Students Early Statistics survey. The data is available at the following link:https://www.officeforstudents.org.uk/data-an...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: General Practitioners: Contracts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, with reference to the Answers of 1 April 2026 to Question 122780 and 25 March 2026 to Question 120988 on General Practitioners: Contracts and with reference to the oral statement of 16 April 2026 o
writtenQuestions.answerText: As per long-standing practice, an Advice and Guidance (A&G) request itself does not begin the formal period of waiting. If a referral is then made, the waiting time is calculated from when the provider receives notice of that referral When providers m...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Maternal Mortality
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether he plans to set an explicit target to close the Black and Asian maternal mortality gap.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is working closely with NHS England, and the wider sector, to identify the right actions and interventions to tackle the stark inequalities that exist for women and babies. Within this, we are committed to setting an explicit target to clos...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: National Maternity and Neonatal Taskforce
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, on which dates the maternity and neonatal task force (a) has met and (b) is due to meet.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The first taskforce meeting took place on the 24 March 2026. A record of this meeting is available at the following link:https://www.gov.uk/government/groups/national-maternity-and-neonatal-taskforce#record-of-meetingsFuture records of meetings will be up...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
